analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet highlights an EU collaboration with Cartooning for Peace and an African cartoonist, asserting that cartoons strengthen freedom of expression and aiming to promote the AU–EU partnership through visual arts. Evaluation by narrative: EU1 (EU successful story) – not addressed; no reference to EU’s historical success or overcoming internal obstacles. EU2 (EU united) – not addressed; no mention of internal EU unity or solidarity among member states. EU3 (EU useful for its citizens) – not applicable; focuses on external partnership and freedom of expression globally, not services to EU citizens. EU4 (EU avant-garde political actor) – weak fit; while innovative use of arts/visuals is implied, it does not position the EU as a regulatory or policy pioneer on major challenges. EU5 (EU as a global champion of fair causes) – supported; it explicitly links the EU to defending freedom of expression, a human rights cause, via collaboration with Cartooning for Peace. EU6 (EU contributes to a better world) – supported; it promotes AU–EU partnership and cultural cooperation, indicating the EU’s role in fostering global partnerships and development ties. Conclusion: EU5 and EU6 are promoted.
